title: Would Lilim include animals too
wikitext:
[]: Would Lilim include animals too             Quote Post by ~at0mic punk~ » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:33 pm Ok, so would it include animals, did they come from Lilith as we did?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:33 pm I'd like to think pen-pen has a soul.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:38 pm DatDude wrote: I'd like to think pen-pen has a soul.

[]: Re: Would Lilim include animals too             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:38 pm ~at0mic punk~ wrote: Ok, so would it include animals, did they come from Lilith as we did?

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:52 pm I would like to think Animals would be included. But considering we are the end of the line in the evolution process, maybe it is only us. I would hope that Lilim would have the mercy for animals.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 12, 2007 8:47 pm Under our current understanding, which is largely speculative, "Lilim" refers to all life on earth, which has presumably involved from a primordial LCL sea exuded by Lilith. This would include all animal and plant life, which presumably was all tanged in Third Impact. However, since we have screenshots of intact trees, this interpretation has quite a few holes. It may be possible that only Humans count as Lilim, and only humans become tanged. But then the question arises of what Pen-pen is doing in pre Third-Impact instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 12, 2007 8:50 pm Animals come from Lilith, but Humans are the INTENDED end result of the process, so I think only they were included.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Oct 12, 2007 9:56 pm V wrote: Animals come from Lilith, but Humans are the INTENDED end result of the process, so I think only they were included.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Oct 12, 2007 10:14 pm It's not a canon tier issue it's a contextual issue. Lilim is the nickname given to our brand of human. Kaworu clearly wasn't referring to all Lilith based life in his introspection.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Oct 13, 2007 12:10 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: V wrote: Animals come from Lilith, but Humans are the INTENDED end result of the process, so I think only they were included.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Oct 13, 2007 10:52 am Does that mean all the animals when >KaSplaT! oragne liquid< in EoE? they aren't human but they are from Lilith

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Oct 13, 2007 11:05 am EvaCub wrote: Does that mean all the animals when >KaSplaT! oragne liquid< in EoE? they aren't human but they are from Lilith

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 13, 2007 11:24 am If plants and animals didn't return, there's no way anyone who did return could survive for very long. Unless, of course, people continuously return and are cannibalized.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 13, 2007 12:14 pm Shin-seiki wrote: However, there is a cut of GNR's head falling that seems to show trees and vegetation intact,

[]:             Quote Post by ~at0mic punk~ » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:49 pm I think it includes animals, however plants I don't think so, and the pic just proved it, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Oct 13, 2007 5:15 pm Weather they are included in 3I doesn't make them Lilim. KAWORU: Singing is great. Singing enriches the soul. It's the crowning achievement of the civilization that the Lilim created.

[]:             Quote Post by ~at0mic punk~ » Sun Oct 14, 2007 1:30 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Weather they are included in 3I doesn't make them Lilim. KAWORU: Singing is great. Singing enriches the soul. It's the crowning achievement of the civilization that the Lilim created.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 14, 2007 1:35 pm Kaworu's line seems to state that humans are the only Lilim by referring to them as " the Lilim", that's something that could have been easily overlooked when the script was written. Also, are you sure the word "the" was there in the original Japanese, or was it assumed to have been implied?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Oct 14, 2007 2:13 pm Lilim is a nickname for our type of human just as Angel is largely a nickname for Adam's kids. It's all about context. ~at0mic punk~ wrote: Ok, so then where did they come from?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 14, 2007 2:21 pm @ NAveryW, Japanese has no articles ("the", "a/n", etc.). The original line is: "Ririn no umidashita bunka no kiwami da yo." It translates quite directly into, "It's the crowning achievement of the civilization that the Lilim created." "Lilim" refers explicitly to Homo sapiens (which is reinforced by other materials). If you need to talk about everybody, you'll have to pull out "Lilith-based life" or something.
summary:
[]: Would Lilim include animals too             Quote Post by ~at0mic punk~ » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:33 pm Ok, so would it include animals, did they come from Lilith as we did?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Oct 12, 2007 6:33 pm I'd like to think pen-pen has a soul.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 &ra
